steps.title: Find each slant height
steps.content:
Each square base has area $64$, so each side length is $8$. The slant height uses half the base side, $4$, and the pyramid height.
$$\ell_1=\sqrt{12^2+4^2}=\sqrt{160}$$
$$\ell_2=\sqrt{24^2+4^2}=\sqrt{592}$$

steps.title: Subtract lateral areas
steps.content:
The bases have equal area, so they cancel in the surface-area difference. For each pyramid, the lateral area is four triangular faces.
$$4(\frac12\cdot8\cdot\ell)=16\ell$$
Using decimal approximations,
$$16(24.33-12.65)=16(11.68)$$
$$16(11.68)=186.88$$

steps.title: Choose the closest value
steps.content: The closest listed value to $186.9$ is $187$. The answer is A.
